_meta.description: Unified save data schema for Wings Over No Man's Land. This file defines the structure and field semantics of the single localStorage object that replaces all fragmented wonml_r2_* keys. Nico: use this as the implementation spec. The migration section defines how to assemble this from existing r2 keys on first load.

version_history.author: Enzo Caruso
version_history.changes: Initial unified save schema. Consolidates all wonml_r2_* fragmented keys into a single JSON object.

schema_version.description: Version of this save data structure. Used by the migration runner to determine if an upgrade is needed. Always write the current schema version when saving.

player_name.description: Pilot name entered by the player. Prefilled on subsequent sessions. Corresponds to wonml_r2_last_pilot.

completed_campaigns.description: Array of campaign IDs that the player has completed. Example: ['belgium', 'marne']. Used by checkUnlocks() to determine which aircraft are available. Corresponds to wonml_r2_completed_campaigns.

unlocked_planes.description: Array of planeType strings explicitly unlocked via campaign rewards. Tier 1 starters are never in this array — they are always available. Example: ['albatrosdvstd', 'dh2']. Corresponds to wonml_r2_unlocked_planes.

hangar_stats.description: Per-aircraft career statistics. Keys are planeType strings. Corresponds to wonml_r2_hangar_stats.

flights.description: Total number of runs started with this aircraft.

kills.description: Total enemy kills across all runs with this aircraft.

best_score.description: Personal best score with this aircraft across all runs and campaigns.

last_flown_at.description: Timestamp of last run with this aircraft. Added in schema v1. Old saves will have null here. NEW FIELD — did not exist in r2.

last_campaign_id.description: Campaign ID of the last run with this aircraft. Enables 'last flown in: Belgium' display in hangar. NEW FIELD — did not exist in r2.

achievements.description: List of achievements the player has earned.

achievement_id.description: Stable ID matching an entry in config/achievements.json.

trigger_context.description: Optional context object recording what triggered the achievement. E.g. { score: 250, campaign_id: 'belgium', plane_type: 'dr1std' }. Useful for display and analytics.

high_scores.description: Per-campaign personal best scores. Keys are campaign IDs. Example: { 'belgium': 312 }. This replaces the global wonml_r2_best and the pilot_bests structure for the unified save. The global all-time best is simply the max value across all entries here.

value_schema.description: Best score ever achieved by this player in this campaign, across all aircraft and runs.

pilot_bests.description: Per-named-pilot best scores (legacy structure from wonml_r2_pilot_bests). Keys are pilot name strings. Retained for compatibility with multi-pilot local play. In the full release, a player_id system should replace this. See migration notes.

value_schema.description: Best score ever recorded under this pilot name.

leaderboard_cache.description: Local cache of the last-fetched Supabase leaderboard. Displayed when offline. Max 10 entries. Corresponds to wonml_r2_leaderboard.

campaign_id.description: Present once new Supabase schema is deployed. Null for old entries.

faction.description: Present once new Supabase schema is deployed. Null for old entries.

settings.description: Player preferences and UI settings. Consolidates wonml_r2_burst_hand and any future settings.

burst_hand.description: Which side of the screen the fire buttons appear on. Corresponds to wonml_r2_burst_hand.

muted.description: Whether BGM is muted. NEW FIELD — mute state is currently not persisted in r2.

early_access.description: Early access gate flag. Value 'ok' means the player has entered a valid code. Corresponds to wonml_r2_early_access. Will be removed when open beta begins.

last_played.description: Timestamp of the last time a run was started. Updated at the beginning of each run. NEW FIELD — did not exist in r2.

total_runs.description: Lifetime total number of runs started. Incremented each time a game begins. NEW FIELD — did not exist in r2. Can be derived on migration by summing all hangar_stats[].flights.

migration.description: Instructions for Nico to implement the migration from fragmented wonml_r2_* keys to the unified wonml_save_v1 key. Run this once on game load before any other save operations. After successful migration, delete the old keys.
migration.trigger_condition: localStorage.getItem('wonml_save_v1') === null AND any wonml_r2_* key exists

steps.description: Read all wonml_r2_* keys from localStorage into a temporary object. Keys to read: wonml_r2_best, wonml_r2_best_pilot, wonml_r2_pilot_bests, wonml_r2_leaderboard, wonml_r2_hangar_stats, wonml_r2_last_pilot, wonml_r2_burst_hand, wonml_r2_early_access, wonml_r2_completed_campaigns, wonml_r2_unlocked_planes.

Build the new unified save object. Map old keys as follows:
- wonml_r2_last_pilot → player_name
- JSON.parse(wonml_r2_completed_campaigns) || [] → completed_campaigns
- JSON.parse(wonml_r2_unlocked_planes) || [] → unlocked_planes
- JSON.parse(wonml_r2_hangar_stats) || {} → hangar_stats (rename 'bestScore' to 'best_score' in each entry; set last_flown_at and last_campaign_id to null)
- JSON.parse(wonml_r2_pilot_bests) || {} → pilot_bests
- JSON.parse(wonml_r2_leaderboard) || [] → leaderboard_cache (existing entries have no campaign_id or faction; leave them null)
- wonml_r2_burst_hand || 'right' → settings.burst_hand
- wonml_r2_early_access → early_access
- achievements: [] (no achievement data existed in r2)
- high_scores: derive from pilot_bests global best and hangar_stats if possible; use wonml_r2_best as the belgium entry if no campaign tracking existed: { belgium: parseInt(wonml_r2_best) || 0 }
- total_runs: sum all hangar_stats[planeType].flights values
- last_played: null (no run timestamp existed in r2)
- schema_version: 1
Write the assembled object to localStorage.setItem('wonml_save_v1', JSON.stringify(save_object)).

Remove all wonml_r2_* keys from localStorage. Also remove legacy ww1_* keys if present: ww1_fokker_best, ww1_fokker_best_pilot, ww1_leaderboard, ww1_last_pilot. Do NOT delete these keys until step 3 has completed successfully.

After deletion, read back wonml_save_v1, JSON.parse it, and verify schema_version === 1 and completed_campaigns is an array. If verification fails, log an error but do not crash — fall back to the newly written object.

migration.future_migration_pattern: For schema v1 → v2: on load, read wonml_save_v1, check save.schema_version. If < 2, run upgradeToV2(save) which adds any new fields with defaults, sets schema_version to 2, and writes to wonml_save_v2. The old wonml_save_v1 key is left for one release cycle then deleted. This pattern repeats for each future version increment.

schema_version_runner_pseudocode.description: Reference implementation for Nico. This is the pattern to follow for all future schema upgrades.
